$comment: ELF64 relocatable + dynamic for AArch64 (Linux / SysV). Ships at the D-LK6-1 sibling cycle (2026-06-01) ‚Äî the closed-enum RelocFormulaKind reshape unblocked ARM64 reloc formula application. Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Section names + flags + ELF identity fields are the SAME values readelf prints; relocation `nativeId` values are the ELF type numbers stored in `r_info` low 32 bits (per the AArch64 ELF ABI, https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa).
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit linux/darwin are LP64 (int 32, long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I64 here. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: Linux arm64 (AAPCS64) uses the generic LSB-first bit-field allocation ‚Äî identical to SysV/GNU gnu_packed for little-endian ‚Äî so it shares the strategy. Byte-ABI-exact (the qemu arm64-ELF corpus is self-consistent under it; the CI cross-compile-compare confirms against the native compiler).
$longDoubleFormatComment: FC17.9(e) D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE: AAPCS64 linux-arm64 `long double` = IEEE binary128, 16/16. See elf64-aarch64-linux-exec.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE + D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE (c145): a relocatable object carries data ‚Äî a global lands in `.rodata`/`.data`/`.bss`/`.data.rel.ro` with `sh_addr=0` + a section-relative `.symtab` entry the FINAL linker binds. Same machine-NEUTRAL elf.cpp ET_REL writer as elf64-x86_64-linux (it reads section names/flags + reloc nativeIds from THIS schema, never hardcoding a machine); arch differs only via config ‚Äî the abs64 data reloc is R_AARCH64_ABS64 (257) here vs R_X86_64_64 (1) on x86_64. A reloc-bearing CONST global (`relro`) emits `.rela.data.rel.ro`; a reloc-bearing MUTABLE pointer emits `.rela.data`. TLS (`tdata`/`tbss`) is deferred to a follow-up.
$externCallDispatchComment: D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH (mirrors x86_64's D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE c141): extern FUNCTION calls in a RELOCATABLE object lower to a PLAIN DIRECT `BL` + a branch relocation against the UNDEFINED extern symbol ‚Äî the SAME call-site shape as the arm64 exec `direct-plt`, except the FINAL (foreign) linker synthesizes the PLT/veneer and resolves the symbol, not DSS. So `direct-plt` is the right dispatch here too; the machine-NEUTRAL elf.cpp ET_REL writer emits the extern as an SHN_UNDEF `.symtab` entry + a `.rela.text` reloc rather than a DSS-synthesized stub. The undefined-extern CALL reloc is R_AARCH64_CALL26 (283) ‚Äî and, UNLIKE x86_64 (which needs the PC32‚ÜíPLT32 `pltNativeId` swap so a foreign `-pie` link routes through a linker PLT), AArch64 has NO distinct PLT-variant branch reloc: `R_AARCH64_CALL26` against an undefined symbol is exactly what gcc/clang emit for `bl printf` in a `.o`, and the linker inserts the veneer/PLT transparently. So the CALL26 reloc row deliberately carries NO `pltNativeId` (the writer emits its plain `nativeId` 283). The arm64 EXEC + macho-arm64-relocatable formats prove this `direct-plt`+CALL26-without-pltNativeId shape.

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity fields. `class` = ELFCLASS64; `data` = ELFDATA2LSB (little-endian ‚Äî AArch64 is little-endian by convention on Linux); `osabi` = ELFOSABI_NONE (SysV); `machine` = EM_AARCH64 (183).

sections.$comment: Executable code. sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS (1); sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (0x6); addralign = 16 (cache-friendly; ARM64 instructions are 4-byte but section alignment is conventionally larger).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Relocation table for .text. sh_type = SHT_RELA (4); sh_flags = SHF_INFO_LINK (0x40); addralign = 8; entrysize = 24 (Elf64_Rela). sh_link/sh_info set per-link.
sections.name: .rela.text

sections.$comment: Symbol table. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); addralign = 8; entrysize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab

sections.$comment: Symbol-name string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addralign = 1.
sections.name: .strtab

sections.$comment: Section-header string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addralign = 1.
sections.name: .shstrtab

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES: empty non-executable-stack marker for foreign-toolchain interop. sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS (1); sh_flags = 0 (NO SHF_EXECINSTR); addralign = 1; size 0. The ELF writer emits it (appended last, ET_REL only) iff this row is present.
sections.name: .note.GNU-stack

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE: read-only initialized data (string literals, const globals). SHT_PROGBITS(1); SHF_ALLOC(2). sh_addr=0 in a relocatable object ‚Äî the final linker assigns the VA. Same machine-neutral writer as x86_64.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE: writable initialized data (mutable globals). SHT_PROGBITS(1); SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3). sh_addr=0 in a relocatable object.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE: zero-initialized data (tentative globals `int g;`). SHT_NOBITS(8) ‚Äî occupies no file bytes. SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3). sh_addr=0 in a relocatable object.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE (c145): relocated-read-only const data ‚Äî a CONST global carrying LOAD-TIME relocations (a const function-pointer table / `int *const p = &x;`). gcc's `.data.rel.ro` contract: SHT_PROGBITS(1) + SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3) (the loader writes the resolved target VA into each slot; a foreign linker places it in GNU_RELRO, read-only after relocation). sh_addr=0 in a relocatable object. The writer emits this section's OWN relocations into `.rela.data.rel.ro` (R_AARCH64_ABS64, abs64) ‚Äî the `.rela.<section>` name is synthesized from this row's name.
sections.name: .data.rel.ro

relocations.$comment: 26-bit PC-relative branch with link / branch (BL / B target). ELF type R_AARCH64_CALL26 = 283. `kind` = 1 matches arm64.target.json's `call26` row.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_CALL26

relocations.$comment: 21-bit PC-relative page-pair (ADRP). ELF type R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21 = 275. `kind` = 2 matches arm64.target.json's `adr_prel_pg_hi21` row.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21

relocations.$comment: 12-bit absolute low (ADD imm12 / LDST imm12). ELF type R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC = 277. `kind` = 3 matches arm64.target.json's `add_abs_lo12_nc` row.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC

relocations.$comment: Absolute 64-bit (data pointer). ELF type R_AARCH64_ABS64 = 257. `kind` = 4 matches arm64.target.json's `abs64` row.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ABS64
